2026-04-22T19:31:49.2614227Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2615346Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2615958Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2616349Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2616664Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2616904Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2617069Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2617253Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-22T19:31:49.2617666Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T19:31:49.2618160Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:31:49.2618418Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}
2026-04-22T19:34:31.9651685Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T19:31:52.002671833Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"description":"Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-22T19:34:31.9730645Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T19:32:43.430408464Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58515,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders, RequestIdMiddleware, and x-request-id in Core README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58396) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58396 introduced the following public API changes to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (targeting `1.0.0-beta.23`):\\n\\n- **Added `PlatformHeaders` static class** — centralizes all platform HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). Replaces per-package `private const` declarations.\\n- **Added `RequestIdMiddleware`** — sets the `x-request-id` response header on every HTTP response (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback). Registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder` and by `AddAgentServerCore()` for Tier 3.\\n- **Added `RequestIdBaggagePropagator`** — propagates `x-request-id` into `Activity.Baggage` for distributed trace correlation.\\n- **Breaking change**: Removed `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` — replaced by `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey`.\\n- **Breaking change**: Replaced 6 individual `Add/Use` extension methods with a single `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair for Tier 3 setups.\\n\\nThe README had only a trivial one-line change (CHANGELOG link update) and does not reflect these new APIs.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a new public type with no entry in the **Key concepts** section of the README — users building Tier 3 setups or referencing header names need to know it exists.\\n- The `x-request-id` response header behavior (added automatically, priority order, error correlation via `additionalInfo.request_id`) is not mentioned anywhere in the README.\\n- The Troubleshooting / Logging section mentions `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` but does not explain the breaking change from the previous six individual extension methods, leaving upgraders without context.\\n- No mention of `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` and how it enables end-to-end distributed trace correlation for Tier 3 standalone setups.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. **Add a `PlatformHeaders` entry to the Key concepts section** (after the existing `FoundryEnvironment` entry):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\nA static class that centralizes all platform HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages:\\n`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, `x-user-isolation-key`, `x-chat-isolation-key`,\\n`traceparent`, and `x-ms-client-request-id`. Use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or\\nisolation-aware logic instead of declaring private string constants.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Upgrading from beta.22?** `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and\\n\\u0026gt; `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` have been removed — use\\n\\u0026gt; `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Add a `Request ID propagation` entry to the Key concepts section** (after `PlatformHeaders`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Request ID propagation\\n\\nEvery HTTP response includes an `x-request-id` header set by `RequestIdMiddleware`. The value is resolved\\nin priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` request header → newly generated GUID. This\\nallows clients to correlate a failed response with backend telemetry. For Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups the\\nmiddleware is registered automatically; for Tier 3 it is included in `AddAgentServerCore()`.\\n\\nError response bodies are enriched with `error.additionalInfo.request_id` matching the header value,\\nenabling client-side error correlation without parsing response headers separately.\\n\\n`RequestIdBaggagePropagator` additionally injects the incoming `x-request-id` value into\\n`Activity.Baggage`, making it available to downstream spans in distributed traces.\\n```\\n\\n3. **Update the Troubleshooting → Logging paragraph** to mention the migration from the old six-method API:\\n\\nReplace the current sentence:\\n\\u0026gt; \\u0026#34;for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it.\\u0026#34;\\n\\nWith:\\n\\u0026gt; \\u0026#34;For Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable request ID propagation,\\n\\u0026gt; version headers, and inbound request logging in a single pair of calls. (Prior to `1.0.0-beta.23`,\\n\\u0026gt; these were six separate `Add/Use` extension methods; those methods have been removed.)\\u0026#34;\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for this change — the concepts are architectural/configuration\\nexplanations that don\\u0026#39;t need test-backed code samples. The existing `Core_ReadMe_CreateBuilder` snippet\\nalready demonstrates the builder pattern.\\n\\nIf a snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage would be helpful, add it in a new sample method in\\n`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/` following the pattern in\\n`sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Start a server)\\n2. ⚠️ Key concepts — missing `PlatformHeaders` and Request ID propagation entries\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ⚠️ Troubleshooting — Logging paragraph needs updating\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected from this docs-only edit\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; setups.\\n\\nThe README had only a trivial one-line change (CHANGELOG link update) and does not reflect these new APIs.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a new public type with no entry in the **Key concepts** section of the README — users building Tier 3 setups or referencing header names need to know it exists.\\n- The `x-request-id` response header behavior (added automatically, step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — `x-chat-isolation-key`,\\n`traceparent`, and `x-ms-client-request-id`. Use `PlatformHeaders` constants in custom middleware or\\nisolation-aware logic instead of declaring private string constants.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Upgrading from beta.22?** `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and\\n\\u0026gt; `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` have been removed — use\\n\\u0026gt; `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` instead.\\n```\\n\\n2. **Add a `Request ID propagation` entry to the Key concepts section** (after `PlatformHeaders`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Request ID propagation\\n\\nEvery HTTP response includes an `x-request-id` header set by `RequestIdMiddleware`. The value is resolved\\nin priority order: OTEL trace ID → incoming `x-request-id` request header → newly generated GUID. This\\nallows clients to correlate a failed response with backend telemetry. For Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups the\\nmiddleware is registered automatically; for Tier 3 it is included in `AddAgentServerCore()`.\\n\\nError response bodies are enriched with `error.additionalInfo.request_id` matching the header value,\\nenabling client-side error correlation without parsing response headers separately.\\n\\n`RequestIdBaggagePropagator` additionally injects the incoming `x-request-id` value into\\n`Activity.Baggage`, making it available to downstream spans in distributed traces.\\n```\\n\\n3. **Update the Troubleshooting → Logging paragraph** to mention the migration from the old six-method API:\\n\\nReplace the current sentence:\\n\\u0026gt; \\u0026#34;for Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable it.\\u0026#34;\\n\\nWith:\\n\\u0026gt; \\u0026#34;For Tier 3, call `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` to enable request ID propagation,\\n\\u0026gt; version headers, and inbound request logging in a single pair of calls. (Prior to `1.0.0-beta.23`,\\n\\u0026gt; these were six separate `Add/Use` extension methods; those methods have been removed.)\\u0026#34;\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for this change — the concepts are architectural/configuration\\nexplanations that don\\u0026#39;t need test-backed code samples. The existing `Core_ReadMe_CreateBuilder` snippet\\nalready demonstrates the builder pattern.\\n\\nIf a snippet showing `PlatformHeaders` usage would be helpful, add it in a new sample method in\\n`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/` following the pattern in\\n`sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/tests/samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs`.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Start a server)\\n2. ⚠️ Key concepts — missing `PlatformHeaders` and Request ID propagation entries\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ⚠️ Troubleshooting — Logging paragraph needs updating\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected from this docs-only edit\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
